headline: Enterprise teams can now sign in with SAML 2.0 SSO, provision users via SCIM, define custom roles, and enjoy faster auth and download performance.

themes.user_facing_summary: Okta and Azure AD SSO is now generally available via SAML 2.0, with certified configurations and a step-by-step setup guide for admins. SCIM 2.0 provisioning lets IT teams create, update, and deactivate users directly from their identity provider.
supporting_changes: FEAT-401: SSO via SAML 2.0 — Okta and Azure AD certified configurations
DOCS-101: SSO setup guide with screenshots for Okta and Azure AD admins

themes.user_facing_summary: Workspace admins can now define their own role permission sets instead of choosing from four fixed presets. Existing workspaces keep their current roles until an admin explicitly opts in.
supporting_changes: FEAT-402: Workspace-level custom role definitions, replacing the 4 fixed presets

themes.user_facing_summary: Authentication is noticeably faster — session caching cuts auth lookup load by ~80% with automatic invalidation on sign-out or password change. Download endpoints are also quicker, with signed-URL generation moved off the request hot path, reducing p99 latency by ~120ms.
supporting_changes: PERF-501: Cache session lookups to reduce auth round-trips by ~80%
PERF-505: Move signed-URL generation out of the request hot path

notable_changes: SAML 2.0 SSO is live with certified Okta and Azure AD configurations; custom IdP setup is available from the workspace SSO settings page.
SCIM 2.0 provisioning supports user create, update, and deactivate; group-based role mapping is planned for a future release.
Custom workspace roles replace the four fixed presets — define exactly the permission set your org needs.
Session cache reduces auth round-trips by ~80% and is invalidated automatically on session revoke or password change.
New SSO setup guide with annotated screenshots removes the SAML-expertise barrier for Okta and Azure AD admins.
customer_voice: Enterprise security teams had been blocking contract signatures on SSO and SCIM support, while customers on non-standard org structures flagged the four fixed role presets as too restrictive. High-traffic customers also called out auth redirect latency and slow large-file downloads as daily friction points.
